CHRISTIAN HEALTH CARE CENTER

CCN: 315376 · WYCKOFF, NJ 07481 · Bergen County

ℹ CCRC

Overview

Address
301 SICOMAC AVE, WYCKOFF, NJ 07481
Phone
2018485200
Certified beds
304
Avg daily residents
277 (91% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit (church-affiliated)
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1997-10-01
Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.01 3.71 4.18 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.82 0.65 0.86 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.66 0.82 0.69 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.53 2.24 2.63 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.48 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.18 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.71
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.56
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.87
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
94.2%
annual
RN turnover
95.8%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.31
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-10-08 16 16 1 88 1 88
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-06-14 9 8 1 52 1 52
Total weighted health score
79.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (43)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
36 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
4 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2024-10-08 2024-10-17
0576 Ensure residents have reasonable access to and privacy in their use of communication methods. E 2024-10-08 2024-10-18
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-11
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-31
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-09
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-11
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-11
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-11
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-10-08 2024-10-31
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-10-08 2024-11-05
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-12
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-31
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-09
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2024-10-08 2024-10-09
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-09
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2024-10-08 2024-10-09
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2023-06-14 2023-06-26
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-06-14 2023-06-15
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2023-06-14 2023-07-28
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-06-14 2023-07-28
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-06-14 2023-07-24
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2023-06-14 2023-07-24
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2023-06-14 2023-06-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-06-14 2023-06-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2023-06-14 2023-04-07
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2021-10-18 2021-11-24
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. B 2021-10-18 2021-11-19
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. K 2021-10-18 2021-11-18
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. K 2021-10-18 2021-11-18
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2021-10-18 2021-11-26
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2021-10-18 2021-12-08
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2021-10-18 2021-12-01
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. G 2021-10-18 2021-12-17
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2021-10-18 2021-11-30
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2021-10-18 2021-12-17
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2021-10-18 2021-12-17
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2021-10-18 2021-12-17
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2021-10-18 2021-12-01
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2021-10-18 2021-11-30
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2021-10-18 2021-12-17
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2021-10-18 2021-12-17
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2021-10-18 2021-11-18
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. L 2021-10-18 2021-11-18

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.

  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
